test_suite.details: node test.js passed all 3519 checks covering wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, data cross-references, sections.json schema, prose content integrity, prediction panels, and Phase A design refresh.

internal_anchors.details: Test suite Section 3 (Internal Links) covers all href='#...' targets and showTab() calls. Full pass — not re-checked manually per instructions.

tab_structure.details: Test suite covers tab structure integrity. Full pass.

bot_blocked: https://www.noaa.gov/ (403 on both attempts — likely bot-blocking, NCEI subpages work fine)
external_links.details: Checked 14 key data source URLs. All NCEI/NOAA data product pages, ESA Gaia, INTERMAGNET, eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov, gracefo.jpl.nasa.gov, spacecenter.dk CHAOS-7, kp.gfz.de, ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/data/poles/NP.xy, dome site pages all return 200. www.noaa.gov root returns 403 on two attempts with and without browser UA — likely CDN bot-blocking, not a broken link. All functional data pages are reachable.

data_prose_consistency.details: Covered by test suite (HTML Output Consistency, Data Cross-References sections). All 3519 tests pass.

win_detail_consistency.details: Covered by test suite. All WIN popups, summary rows, verdict classes verified.

win_dome_alignment.explanation: Our wins.json has 71 entries (WIN-001 through WIN-069 plus WIN-057b and WIN-058b). The dome site claims 69 confirmed wins. The 2-entry delta is accounted for by our WIN-057b and WIN-058b sub-entries — these are analytical split entries we added for precision that the dome does not separately number. No title/claim drift or number collision detected in the sections of wins.html reviewed.
win_dome_alignment.details: Fetched dome wins.html (69 WINs confirmed). PROS-001 through PROS-008 are aliases with cross-references (e.g., PROS-001 = WIN-004). WIN-010 and WIN-025 removed from dome's confirmed list per PROS-003 suspension note; both are tracked in our wins.json (expected — we document dome claims including removed/suspended ones). No unexpected renumbering observed.

progressive_disclosure.details: Covered by test suite. TLDRs, details/summary structure, win sections all verified.

hardcoded_colors.details: Scanned data/sections.json for hardcoded light-theme colors (#fff, #ffffff, #f8f9fa, etc.) in background/color declarations. Zero matches found. ISS-1078 (minor, hardcoded backgrounds) may be resolved — the colors it flagged appear absent in current sections.json.

discoverability.sitemap_xml: present, valid urlset root, contains main URL
discoverability.robots_txt: present, has Allow:/ and Sitemap: directive
discoverability.uncounted_failures: present, 10 entries, no duplicate IDs, all required fields present
discoverability.meta_tags: meta description: yes, og:title: yes, og:description: yes, ClaimReview JSON-LD: yes
discoverability.details: All discoverability infrastructure present and well-formed.

expansion_tracker.id_gap_detail: EXP-052 missing from both live items[] and archive. EXP-051 and EXP-053 both present. No EXP-052.json file in expansions/ directory. No open ISS tracking this gap.

orphaned_files.assessment: No pure EXP-NNN.json orphans (0). The 76 legacy-format files (EXP-NNN-description.json) predate the current tracker naming convention and represent old-style expansion outputs that were never back-filled into the tracker. The 95 non-EXP files (ISS-*, PRED-assessment-*, MODE5-*, etc.) are analyst research artifacts stored in the expansions/ directory — not tracker records. Neither category represents truly lost work, but the mixed directory content makes the standard orphan check misleading.

expansion_tracker.details: EXP-052 gap is the only genuinely concerning finding. next_id=295 is correct (>max 294). Live items: EXP-190 (draft_awaiting_human_group_review), EXP-293 (complete), EXP-294 (pending).

workspace_only_files.details: Spot-checked 14 most recently modified review and expansion files (last 48h) against GitHub API. All confirmed present in git (HTTP 200 from GitHub contents API). Recent files: WIN-059.c3.json, SEC-8b.3.c8.json, WIN-018.c4.json, WIN-036.c5.json, EXP-289 through EXP-293 — all in git.

section_new_queue_coupling.details: No section-new or category-proposal-writeup items in live tracker with status != integrated. Nothing to couple.

workspace_sync_skips.details: 82 mtime-guard skips in last 24h — all expected (monitor/analyst/category-proposals/CAT-005.json, monitor/decisions daily reports, applied-patches, processed-reviews, closed-issues). No direction violations. System is healthy.

digest_coverage.details: Fresh digest regenerated. 0 parse errors. 1 unprocessed file (WIN-059.c3.json, created 2026-05-07T22:44Z) — normal pipeline lag, decider hasn't run since curmudgeon wrote it. Digest reports 4 pending reviews with severity breakdown: 0 critical, 1 major, 0 moderate, 3 minor.

drift_audit.details: drift-audit.json is 52.3h old — fresh enough, no refresh needed. 20 curmudgeon review candidates flagged for change-detection review.

build_reproducibility.details: Ran node build.js html and diffed against published docs/index.html. Single 2-line diff (4 lines total counting diff metadata): the meta date stamp on line 343 shows 'May 7, 2026' in the published file vs 'May 8, 2026' in the freshly built output. This is expected — build.js always stamps today's date; the published file was last built yesterday. No data drift. Restored original after check.
build_reproducibility.note: Build ran in workspace (not a fresh clone). /tmp filesystem is 100% full — fresh-clone method impossible until disk is cleaned.

disk_space.details: /tmp is completely full (9.6G/9.6G). The partial dome-model-review clone attempted during this run ran out of space and left a partial clone at /tmp/dome-integrity-clone (only ~40MB of metadata was written before failure). Prior session clones may have accumulated in /tmp. Sessions filesystem (/sessions) has 1.1G remaining — adequate for current operations but worth monitoring. /tmp fullness means any agent trying to clone there will fail.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 is missing from both the live expansion tracker items[] and the expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl. EXP-051 and EXP-053 both exist in the archive. No EXP-052.json file in monitor/analyst/expansions/. This is a genuine ID gap indicating the entry was either never written or was lost. No open ISS tracks this gap.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate git log for any commit that created EXP-052; if lost, the gap can be permanently recorded by adding a sentinel entry in the archive with status='lost' and a note. The ID is not recoverable but the gap should be acknowledged to prevent confusion.

issues_found.description: monitor/analyst/expansions/ directory contains 171 files not matched by the standard orphan check (tracker live + archive). Breakdown: 76 legacy-format EXP-NNN-description.json files (pre-tracker naming convention, not back-filled into tracker) + 95 non-EXP files (ISS-*, PRED-assessment-*, MODE5-*, etc. stored in this directory). No pure EXP-NNN.json orphans detected (count = 0). This is a directory hygiene / check false-positive issue, not lost work.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Low priority. Consider moving ISS-* and PRED-* research files to appropriate subdirectories (e.g., monitor/analyst/research/) to reduce orphan check noise. Legacy EXP-NNN-description.json files are historical — no action needed unless disk pressure grows.

issues_found.description: https://www.noaa.gov/ (root domain) returns HTTP 403 on two automated attempts, both with and without a browser User-Agent. All NCEI data product subpages (ncei.noaa.gov/products/*) and ngdc.noaa.gov paths return 200. This is consistent with CDN bot-blocking on the homepage, not a real outage.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action needed. If this persists for 3+ runs, consider replacing the www.noaa.gov root link with a more specific data page link.

issues_found.description: /tmp filesystem is 100% full. A partial integrity clone was left at /tmp/dome-integrity-clone from this run (could not complete checkout). Any agent attempting to clone in /tmp will fail with 'no space left on device'. Build reproducibility check used workspace rebuild workaround successfully.
issues_found.suggested_fix: This is a sandbox infrastructure issue outside agent control. Noting for operator awareness. Sessions filesystem has 1.1G remaining — adequate. /tmp accumulates from prior sessions and cannot be cleaned across session boundaries.

informational.item: ISS-1078 (hardcoded light colors) may be resolved
informational.detail: ISS-1078 tracks hardcoded #f5f5f5 backgrounds in sections.json. Current scan of sections.json found 0 hardcoded light colors. Either ISS-1078 was fixed and not closed, or the pattern it tracked has been removed. Decider should verify and close if resolved.

informational.item: Digest pending review: WIN-059.c3.json (major severity finding)
informational.detail: The freshly regenerated digest shows 1 pending review at major severity: WIN-059.c3.json. This review flags an internal contradiction in WIN-059's detail_verdict_text about whether NMP drift rate is monitored. Decider should process on next run.

informational.item: Live tracker EXP-190 in draft_awaiting_human_group_review status
informational.detail: EXP-190 has been in draft_awaiting_human_group_review for an extended period. Not stale by the 48h pending threshold (different status), but flagging for operator awareness.

informational.item: WIN-057b and WIN-058b explain dome vs our WIN count
informational.detail: Our wins.json has 71 entries vs dome's claimed 69. The 2 extra are WIN-057b and WIN-058b — analyst sub-entries not separately counted by the dome. This is expected and not a discrepancy.

summary: Site structure is healthy: all 3519 tests pass, build reproducibility confirmed (single-line date-stamp diff, expected), discoverability infrastructure intact, external data source links reachable, no workspace-only files at risk, digest clean (0 parse errors), workspace-sync operating normally. One major finding: EXP-052 is missing from the expansion tracker — both live and archive — with no file on disk and no ISS tracking it. This is a genuine ID gap that should be acknowledged. One moderate finding: the expansions/ directory contains 171 files not matched by the tracker orphan check, but investigation shows zero pure EXP-NNN.json orphans; the unmatched files are legacy-format (76) and non-EXP research artifacts (95) — directory hygiene issue, not lost work. Minor: www.noaa.gov root returns 403 (bot-blocking, data subpages fine). Infrastructure note: /tmp disk is 100% full, preventing clone-based build checks in future runs — build reproducibility check worked via workspace rebuild workaround.
